notes: mode=strict makes every source commit and explicit close require runtime evidence.
mode=calibrated applies the hard rule to active units whose riskTier is listed in strictRiskTiers.
Host-private hook transport may collect signals, but policy and bypass audit state remain project-local.
In strict mode the workspace-writable signal ledger is diagnostic only: the completion boundary reruns every executable command from verificationContract before allowing a source commit.

verificationContract is pinned to the canonical .itd/VERIFICATION_CONTRACT.json path and must match HEAD plus the last source-code checkpoint; changing the oracle after code work requires an explicit audited human bypass.
SESSION_EXIT_CONTRACT.json must point to the same canonical verifier so strict policy, baseline validation, and explicit close execute one oracle.
